title: AirREGI vs Square POS: Which Free POS Fits Your Store?
metaTitle: AirREGI vs Square POS: Which Free POS Fits Your Store?
description: Compare AirREGI and Square POS across free features, hardware, payments, inventory, customer management, and expansion options.
lead: AirREGI and Square POS both provide a free standard POS application. AirREGI emphasizes Japanese store workflows and the Air service ecosystem, while Square emphasizes integrated payments, hardware, online commerce, and invoices.
author: Luqevora.com Editorial Team

ctas.label: Check current Square POSレジ terms

sources.label: Airレジ — 機能・できること

sections.heading: Verdict: choose the surrounding service ecosystem
body: AirREGI fits a Japanese store that wants to build around AirPAY, reservations, accounting connections, and restaurant ordering.
Square POS fits a business that wants Square payments, hardware, online selling, and invoices under one platform.

sections.heading: Free scope and startup cost
body: Both standard POS applications are free, but a production store still needs devices, printing, cash handling, payments, and connectivity.
AirREGI can use existing compatible iPads, while Square offers published Reader, Stand, and Terminal options.

rows: Square payments in the same account
Catalog, inventory, customers, sales, accounting links
Catalog, inventory, customers, sales, online, invoices
Square for Restaurants and kitchen tools
Free POS plus peripherals and services
Free POS plus hardware and industry plans

sections.heading: Register functions
body: AirREGI documents order entry, checkout, inventory, customers, sales analysis, CSV exports, accounting connections, and offline checkout.
Square provides catalog, inventory, customers, reports, staff access, and refunds and connects those functions to online and invoice workflows.

sections.heading: Payment integration
body: AirREGI transfers amounts to AirPAY to reduce duplicate entry, but the payment contract and underwriting remain separate.
Square POS and Square payments operate under the same provider and account, simplifying hardware and payment reporting.

sections.heading: Advanced restaurant and retail functions
body: AirREGI Order, Square for Restaurants, retail plans, and other advanced modules can create additional software or hardware cost.
Do not compare only the free applications when handhelds, kitchen displays, mobile ordering, multi-store administration, or detailed inventory are required.

sections.heading: Run the same scenario
body: Configure one week of products, staff, discounts, refunds, closing, inventory, and payments in both systems and reproduce the same trading scenario.

A practical pilot should use the same products, discounts, tax rules, staff roles, refund cases, and end-of-day reports in both systems. Record setup time, checkout time, correction steps, hardware needs, and export quality. This converts a feature comparison into an operating-cost comparison and exposes training or exception-handling differences before a full migration. Rehearse the selected workflow with every role before launch.

faqs.question: Should I simply choose the lowest processing rate?
faqs.answer: No. Compare the effective cost including eligibility, payment mix, hardware, subscriptions, settlement, and integration.

faqs.question: Should POS and payments come from one provider?
faqs.answer: One provider can simplify amount transfer and support, but also compare functionality, rates, and portability.

faqs.question: Which sales data is needed for a comparison?
faqs.answer: Prepare at least three months of value and transaction count by card brand, e-money, QR, cash, and in-person or online channel.
